question: Is the image presented from a top-down angle showing an archaeological restoration laboratory?
Are distinct foreground, midground, and background layers clearly visible?
Is there a damaged blue-and-white porcelain plate (white base, blue patterns, chips/cracks) in the center foreground?
Are there exactly 12 ceramic shards arranged in a clockwise ring around the central plate?
Do the ceramic shards have small white stickers numbered 01 through 12?
Does shard 04 overlap shard 03, specifically covering a corner of shard 03?
Is shard 07 leaning vertically against a gray foam block?
Is shard 11 half-covered by the shadow of the plate's rim?
Are there exactly 6 fine brushes in the left foreground, arranged parallel and ordered from thick to thin?
Is the tip of the thinnest brush pointing toward the center of the plate?
Does the stainless steel tray contain exactly 3 cotton swabs, 2 disposable blades, and 1 eraser?
Is the eraser in the tray stamped with the text '2B'?
Is there an electronic scale (black panel) in the right foreground displaying '125.0g'?
Does the scale have a label on the front edge reading 'CAL'?
Is there a semi-transparent weighing boat containing white powder sitting on the scale?
Are there two tweezers (one curved-tip, one straight-tip) crossed, with the curved one pressing down on the straight one?
Are there exactly 6 transparent evidence bags (with red seal tape) in the midground?
Do the evidence bags have labels reading 'E1' through 'E6'?
Does bag E3 contain 2 copper coins, bag E4 contain a black USB drive, and bag E6 contain a small glass bottle?
Is there a ring magnifying lamp behind the evidence bags casting a circular highlight?
Are there exactly 3 people in the midground (restorer, assistant, photographer)?
Is the restorer wearing blue nitrile gloves and holding ceramic shard 08 with their left hand?
Is the assistant on the right wearing a white lab coat and holding a clipboard titled 'RESTORATION LOG'?
Is the photographer on the left holding a camera aimed at the plate, with their shoulder partially obscuring the magnifying lamp stand?
Are there exactly 4 kraft paper archive boxes on the background shelving with 'FRAGILE' printed on the sides?
Is there a row of at least 8 glass reagent bottles on the shelving, alternating between brown and clear?
Does the whiteboard on the wall display the text 'Project A, Steps 1-3'?
Does the digital clock in the corner display '14:20'?
Is the image free from severe artifacts (e.g., floating objects not specified, melting objects)?
